title: Our Visitor
content:
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s a fellow on the station
آ آ آ آ (He dropped in on a call,
آ آ آ آ Just casual to stay a pleasant week),
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s a bankerï؟½s near relation,
آ آ آ آ Strongly built, and very tall,
آ آ آ آ Not altogether destitute of cheek;
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s a descent judge of whisky,
آ آ آ آ And the hardest working youth
آ آ آ آ Who ever played a polo on a cob;
آ آ آ آ His anecdotes are risky,
آ آ آ آ And to tell the honest truth,
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s waiting here until he gets a job.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s waiting, as I mention,
آ آ آ آ And wheneï؟½er he says his prayers,
آ آ آ آ Which he doesnï؟½t do as frequently as some,
آ آ آ آ And I fear that his intention
آ آ آ آ Isnï؟½t quite so good as theirs
آ آ آ آ For he prays to God the work may never come.
آ آ آ آ He marches with the banner
آ آ آ آ Of the noble unemployed,
آ آ آ آ He mixes with the fashionable mob,
آ آ آ آ But while heï؟½s got a tanner
آ آ آ آ He scorns to be decoyed
آ آ آ آ Where thereï؟½s any chance he may get a job.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s an excellent musician,
آ آ آ آ And the song that suits him best,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Old Stumpyï؟½ is a masterpiece of art;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis a splendid composition
آ آ آ آ As he chucks it off his chest,
آ آ آ آ Though thereï؟½s something of a hitch about the start.
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s an artist, too, in colours
آ آ آ آ For he painted up the boat.
آ آ آ آ You wonder but he did, so help me bob,
آ آ آ آ And all the champion scullers,
آ آ آ آ When once he gets afloat,
آ آ آ آ Couldnï؟½t catch him if they offered him a job.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s very unpretending,
آ آ آ آ Most affable and kind,
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll take a whisky any time it suits;
آ آ آ آ Extremely condescending,
آ آ آ آ He really does not mind,
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll even, when itï؟½s muddy, wear your boots.
آ آ آ آ Some think he isnï؟½t clever,
آ آ آ آ But itï؟½s my distinct belief
آ آ آ آ That thereï؟½s much more than they fancy in his nob.
آ آ آ آ But heï؟½s travelling on the ï؟½neverï؟½
آ آ آ آ And will surely die of grief
آ آ آ آ On the day when heï؟½s compelled to take a job.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Barcroft Boake represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Barcroft Boake's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
